_comment: OpenCode adapter assembly spec. Tells gen/generate.py how to build the full
.opencode/ adapter (+ the root AGENTS.md) from the SAME neutral source as the
Claude adapter — no re-carving of any body. The generator stays THIN (arch
note 'How thin the metalanguage can be'): it inlines a neutral body, prepends
the OpenCode frontmatter from this manifest, and copies the config + entry
surface artifacts verbatim. No parsing, no re-serialization — so the output is
deterministic (same inputs -> byte-identical outputs).
Frontmatter shape per https://opencode.ai/docs/agents/ : `description` +
`mode: subagent` + a `tools` OBJECT map (NOT Claude's comma-list). Agent
files land under .opencode/agent/ (the filename is the agent id); command
files under .opencode/command/. The always-on core is wired via the
`instructions` array in opencode.json (https://opencode.ai/docs/config/), the
OpenCode analogue of the Claude @-import.
PREVIEW: the model setup below is a PROVISIONAL preview default. The `models`
block carries a SINGLE value — `session` — which the generator substitutes
into opencode.json's top-level `model` (the session default). EVERY generated
agent inherits that session model; the adapter bakes NO per-agent `model:`/
`variant:` pin into any frontmatter. The control-layer override (the reviewer
model governing the four checking agents) is NOT a template pin — it is a
four-line personal-config block in the PM's own opencode.json (see the `models`
_comment + doc/stack-notes.md). Changing the session model later = edit the one
value below + regenerate; no per-file editing. DeepSeek is the only
authenticated provider here, so v4-pro is a PREVIEW default — downstream
overrides for their own provider.
Each section names the harness-neutral capability (see
src/manifests/capabilities.json) it realizes, so the same neutral bodies are
re-targeted to OpenCode without touching src/agents|commands/*.body.md.
SINGLE session-model default (PROVISIONAL preview default, PM 2026-06-08 —
DeepSeek is the only authenticated provider here). `session` is the ONLY
model this block carries: the generator substitutes it into opencode.json's
top-level `model` (https://opencode.ai/docs/config/), which becomes the
session/primary default. EVERY generated agent — producers, the orchestrator,
the review/audit layer, the engines — inherits this session model by default;
the adapter bakes NO per-agent `model:`/`variant:` pin into any frontmatter.
Override this one value for a different provider, then regenerate.
CONTROL-LAYER MODEL (the reviewer model governs the checking agents) — NOT a
template pin. The four checking agents (code-review, pm-auditor,
pm-plan-checker, pm-product-advocate) run on the session model unless the PM
sets a reviewer model. OpenCode has NO native cross-agent model inheritance
and no runtime per-task model override (PR #17577 closed-not-merged,
https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/pull/17577), so there is no one-line
knob. The control-layer override lives in the PM's OWN opencode.json as a
four-line personal-config block (one `agent.<id>.model` entry per checking
agent) — NOT here in the template. That keeps it surviving a protocol bump
(the generator never touches keys it does not own) and absent => all four
inherit the session. The canonical block + rationale live in
doc/stack-notes.md (OpenCode § control-layer model) and are pointed at from
AGENTS.md; verified 1.16.2 that a config-level `agent.<id>.{model,variant}`
override is applied and a `mode:all`/subagent inherits the session.
OpenCode-ONLY engine subagents. On Claude the protocol delegates
technical-quality review to the BUILT-IN `code-review` engine and deep
research to the BUILT-IN `deep-research` engine. OpenCode ships NO built-in
equivalents (verified OpenCode 1.16.2: built-in agents are only
build/plan/general/explore — opencode.ai/docs/agents/), so the adapter must
SHIP its own review + research subagents for the orchestrator to delegate to.
These are OpenCode-only — Claude keeps its built-ins, so they are NOT added
to the Claude adapter and are NOT shared neutral bodies.
Distinct from the shared `agents` section: there the body is neutral (read
from src/agents/) and only the frontmatter is per-harness. Here BOTH the body
AND the frontmatter live under src/manifests/opencode/ (body_dir +
frontmatter_dir below) — they are harness-specific engine bodies. Same thin
assembly (frontmatter + body, byte-copied) and same output dir (.opencode/
agent/, filename = agent id), so single-source-diff-clean covers them too.
Naming: the agent ids stay `code-review` and `deep-research` (the filename is
the agent id) so they match the protocol's delegation points. The enforcement
plugin's wb-* deny-list does NOT include them — they are ALLOWED engines, the
same way code-review/deep-research are not gated on Claude.
ORCHESTRATOR (`ai-pm`): also harness-local — a `mode: primary` agent carrying
the orchestrator IDENTITY as its system prompt. On Claude the orchestrator IS
the main session (its system prompt is the project entry file), so no agent file
is generated there; on OpenCode the default `build` primary is a do-it-all coder
that authors content itself, so the adapter SHIPS a first-class orchestrator
primary whose tool grant DENIES write/edit (content authoring is impossible —
it must delegate to pm-coder/pm-architect). It is made the default primary via
the top-level `default_agent` key in opencode.json (below). See the
opencode-orchestrator-primary plan.
Model: NOT pinned in these .fm files — and not pinned anywhere by the
adapter. Every harness-local agent (the `code-review` engine, `deep-research`,
the `ai-pm` orchestrator) inherits the session model. The control-layer model
(the reviewer model governing `code-review` + the three checking pm-* agents)
is a four-line personal-config block in the PM's OWN opencode.json, NOT a
template pin (OpenCode has no native cross-agent model inheritance / runtime
per-task override, PR #17577 unmerged). Absent that block, all four inherit
the session. See doc/stack-notes.md (OpenCode § control-layer model).
The OpenCode CORE enforcement plugin. Emitted from a template
(opencode/plugin/<name>.js.tmpl) with the wb-* role deny-list injected by
the generator FROM the Claude settings.json Task|Agent|Skill matcher (the
single authored copy of that role set). The __WB_DENY_ROLES__ placeholder
in the template is replaced with the extracted role array — so there is
exactly one authored copy and the two adapters cannot drift. Per
https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/ : tool.execute.before throws to deny.
Verified subagent-effective on OpenCode 1.16.2 (Spike B, 2026-06-07).
REGISTRATION: the file AUTO-LOADS from .opencode/plugin/ — confirmed on the
real loader (opencode debug config --pure lists it in the resolved plugin
set with NO opencode.json entry). An explicit `plugin` array entry would
DOUBLE-register the same file (auto-load + config), so opencode.json
deliberately carries no plugin entry.
The source carries a `__SESSION_MODEL__` placeholder in its top-level
`model` key; the generator substitutes it with models.session (the single
source). This is the OpenCode session/primary default — a PROVISIONAL
preview default (rationale lives in this adapter.json's _comment; it cannot
go in opencode.json, which OpenCode validates as strict JSON with no
unrecognized keys).
